name: IPSCO Inc.
name_former: Interprovincial Steel and Pipe Corporation Ltd.
description: IPSCO Inc. was a leading Canadian steel and pipe manufacturer headquartered in Regina, Saskatchewan. Founded in 1956 as Interprovincial Steel and Pipe Corporation Ltd., the company specialized in producing steel plate, coil, and tubular products, primarily serving the energy, construction, and manufacturing sectors across North America. IPSCO operated steelworks and pipe mills in both Canada and the United States before being acquired by Swedish steelmaker SSAB in 2007, with its tubular operations subsequently sold to Evraz and TMK.
